column4: OpenRAVE began as a rewrite of the RAVE simulator (developed by James Kuffner, 1995) by Rosen Diankov at CMU in 2006. It uses a plugin architecture for planners, controllers, and sensors. Stable release is 0.8.2 (2012); current GitHub code is more recent but formal versioning lags. Widely used in manipulation and grasping research. A Python 3 compatible fork (openrave3) exists on GitHub.

column4: Installation is complex and time-consuming. Requires building from source with many system dependencies. The documentation is fragmented across the official website and community wikis. Compatibility with newer Ubuntu versions (22.04+) requires additional patches. Docker-based installation is a simpler alternative but requires Docker knowledge. This is one of the more difficult installations among the simulators reviewed.

column4: Highly modular plugin architecture: planners, controllers, sensors, and IK solvers are all plugins. IKFast is a notable sub-tool for generating fast analytical IK solutions. Python API allows scripting and integration with other systems. Supports COLLADA and URDF robot models.
